skill.name: vm-template-creation
skill.description: Create, configure, and manage reusable VM templates in Proxmox for standardized infrastructure deployment.
skill.summary: Build reusable Proxmox VM templates for repeatable server, Kubernetes, and application deployments.
skill.author: DataKnifeAI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and Ruby backtick execution patterns, but manual review found Markdown examples and Proxmox MCP tool names rather than executable code. No evidence found of cryptographic implementation, command execution, network exfiltration, prompt injection, or malicious intent. The remaining concern is operational risk because the skill guides users through VM creation, cloning, configuration updates, and deletion.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Matches
low_findings.description: The referenced lines describe VM templates, Cloud-Init workflows, and Kubernetes node cloning. No weak hash, cipher, password storage algorithm, key handling, or cryptographic API usage was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines found VM workflow text and generated marketplace copy, not cryptographic code. The static pattern appears to match substrings in prose or identifiers.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Backtick Command Execution Pattern Matches
low_findings.description: The backtick findings are Markdown formatting for tool names, configuration parameters, and fenced examples. They are not Ruby source code and do not execute shell commands by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown documentation, and the flagged syntax appears inside lists or examples. No executable Ruby, shell wrapper, script file, or dynamic command construction was found.

low_findings.title: Operational Infrastructure Change Risk
low_findings.description: The skill references actions that can create, update, clone, stop, shut down, or delete Proxmox VMs. These are legitimate for the skill, but users should review target node names, VM IDs, and deletion steps before execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited tool names and workflows clearly describe infrastructure-changing operations. The risk is contextual and operational, not evidence of malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: Manual VM template creation can produce slow, inconsistent server builds. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through repeatable Proxmox template workflows.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation of base Proxmox VMs for template conversion.
Shows how to mark configured VMs as reusable templates.
Provides clone workflows for standardized VM deployment.
Documents Cloud-Init considerations for network and host setup.
Outlines Kubernetes and RKE2 template deployment patterns.
Explains template update, testing, naming, and lifecycle practices.
limitations: It provides guidance and MCP tool examples, not a standalone Proxmox client.
It does not import disk images automatically.
Templates must be cloned before they can be booted as normal VMs.
Users must verify VM IDs, storage names, and network bridges in their environment.

use_cases.title: Standardize Kubernetes Nodes
use_cases.description: Create RKE2-ready Ubuntu templates, then clone them into repeatable control plane and worker nodes.

use_cases.title: Accelerate Server Provisioning
use_cases.description: Build reusable templates for web, database, and CI runner servers with consistent baseline settings.

use_cases.title: Prepare IaC Base Images
use_cases.description: Use interactive MCP workflows to prepare templates before scaling deployments with Terraform.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Ubuntu Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Proxmox Ubuntu 22.04 template plan for node pve2 with 2 CPU cores, 4 GB memory, and a 50 GB disk.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a basic reusable Linux template.

prompt_templates.title: Convert VM to Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Review VM 100 on node pve2 and guide me through the checks before marking it as a reusable template.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request to safely convert a prepared VM.

prompt_templates.title: Clone Kubernetes Nodes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use template VM 100 to plan three full clones for RKE2 control plane nodes with unique names and VM IDs.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a multi-node Kubernetes workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Update Template Lifecycle
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a safe workflow to clone an existing template, apply patches, test it, create a new template version, and retire the old one.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced maintenance workflow for production templates.

output_examples.input: Create a basic Ubuntu template for Proxmox.
output: A template plan with node, VM ID, CPU, memory, disk, storage, and network bridge choices.
A checklist for installing packages, applying patches, enabling Cloud-Init, and removing sensitive data.
A final conversion step that marks the tested VM as a template.

output_examples.input: Clone a template into three Kubernetes nodes.
output: A clone plan with unique VM IDs and hostnames for each node.
Guidance for full clone mode, Cloud-Init networking, and post-clone validation.
A reminder to verify cluster health after RKE2 initialization.

output_examples.input: Update an existing application server template.
output: A maintenance workflow that clones the old template to a temporary VM.
A testing checklist for patches, services, and baseline configuration.
A versioning plan for promoting the new template and retiring the old one.

best_practices: Use Cloud-Init for hostnames, users, keys, and network settings.
Remove credentials, tokens, shell history, and temporary files before templating.
Test every template by cloning and booting a validation VM.
anti_patterns: Do not convert a VM to a template before patching and testing it.
Do not reuse VM IDs, hostnames, or static IP addresses across clones.
Do not delete old templates until replacement clones are validated.

faq.question: Does this skill execute Proxmox commands directly?
faq.answer: No. It provides workflow guidance and MCP tool examples for an AI assistant to use with your approval.

faq.question: Can templates be started after conversion?
faq.answer: No. Proxmox templates are cloned first, and the cloned VMs are started.

faq.question: Can it create Kubernetes-ready templates?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes an RKE2-oriented Ubuntu template workflow and clone planning for cluster nodes.

faq.question: Does it handle Cloud-Init networking?
faq.answer: It explains Cloud-Init setup considerations, but users must provide environment-specific network values.

faq.question: Can it replace Terraform?
faq.answer: No. It helps prepare and test templates. Terraform remains useful for repeatable production deployments.

faq.question: What should I check before deleting a template?
faq.answer: Confirm that replacement templates are tested, dependent clones are known, and the VM ID is correct.
